Arbeitsrecht
Fragen rund ums Arbeitsrecht in der Schweiz begegnen Unternehmen laufend â etwa bei KÃ¼ndigungen, Ãberstunden oder Ferien. Damit Sie rechtlich auf der sicheren Seite sind, arbeiten wir bei allen arbeitsrechtlichen Anliegen mit erfahrenen Jurist:innen aus unserem Partnernetzwerk zusammen. Sie erhalten fundierte Antworten, kombiniert mit unserer praxisnahen Umsetzung in der Personaladministration. So verbinden wir rechtliche Expertise mit effizienter Beratung â individuell und zuverlÃ¤ssig.
Unsere detaillierten Dienstleistungen
Wir begleiten Sie mit unserer Erfahrung – und ziehen bei Bedarf spezialisierte Fachpersonen aus unserem Netzwerk bei.
Ersteinschätzung bei arbeitsrechtlichen Fragen aus dem Alltag
Unterstützung bei der rechtssicheren Umsetzung von Arbeitszeit- und Ferienregelungen
Beratung bei Vertragsauflösungen und Kündigungen
Zusammenarbeit mit Fachanwälten für Arbeitsrecht Schweiz bei komplexen Fällen
Anpassung Ihrer internen Dokumente gemäss arbeitsrechtlicher Vorgaben
Einen Arbeitsvertrag richtig erstellen
Rechtliche Klarheit schafft Vertrauen – intern wie extern. Wir begleiten Sie verlässlich und leiten Sie bei Bedarf an die passenden Expert:innen weiter.
Verlässlicher Erstkontakt für alle Fragen rund ums Arbeitsrecht
Zugriff auf unser erprobtes Partnernetzwerk mit Spezialist:innen
Kombination aus rechtlicher Expertise und administrativer Umsetzung
Schnelle Reaktionszeiten bei dringenden Fällen
Dokumente und Prozesse auf dem aktuellen Stand des Schweizer Arbeitsrechts
Wissenswertes
Schon gewusst?
In der Schweiz gilt das Obligationenrecht (OR) als Grundlage des Arbeitsrechts. Zusätzlich gelten je nach Branche Gesamtarbeitsverträge (GAV), kantonale Bestimmungen oder betriebsinterne Regelungen. Eine rechtlich saubere Grundlage schützt Sie im Streitfall – etwa bei Kündigungen, Abmahnungen oder Streitigkeiten rund um Überstunden. Unsere Zusammenarbeit mit externen Fachanwälten stellt sicher, dass Sie jederzeit korrekt und nachvollziehbar handeln.
Jetzt Offerte anfragen
HÃ¤ufig gestellte Fragen
Was umfasst Lohnbuchhaltung?
Die Lohnbuchhaltung umfasst sämtliche Tätigkeiten im Zusammenhang mit der Entlöhnung von Mitarbeitenden. Dazu gehören:
Berechnung und Auszahlung der Löhne
Abrechnung von Sozialversicherungen (AHV, IV, ALV, BVG, UVG)
Quellensteuerabrechnungen
Erstellung von Lohnabrechnungen und Lohnausweisen
Verwaltung von Ferien, Überstunden und Abwesenheiten
Jahresendverarbeitung und Meldungen an Behörden
Eine korrekte Lohnbuchhaltung ist entscheidend für die Einhaltung gesetzlicher Vorgaben und das Vertrauen der Mitarbeitenden. Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich übernimmt diese Aufgaben effizient und zuverlässig.
Was ist der Unterschied zwischen Buchhaltung und Lohnbuchhaltung?
Die Buchhaltung bildet die finanzielle Gesamtlage eines Unternehmens ab. Sie umfasst die Erfassung von Geschäftsvorfällen wie Einnahmen, Ausgaben, Investitionen und die Erstellung von Bilanz und Erfolgsrechnung.
Die Lohnbuchhaltung ist ein spezieller Teilbereich der Buchhaltung. Sie bezieht sich ausschliesslich auf die Verwaltung der Löhne und die Abwicklung aller lohnrelevanten Sozialabgaben und Abrechnungen.
Kurz gesagt: Die Buchhaltung steuert das Finanzwesen, die Lohnbuchhaltung das Personalwesen – beide gehören zum Kerngeschäft eines Treuhänders.
Was ist Payrolling?
Payrolling bedeutet die vollständige Auslagerung der Lohnadministration an einen externen Dienstleister wie das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich. Der Treuhänder übernimmt die gesamte Lohnverarbeitung inklusive Lohnabrechnungen, Sozialversicherungsabrechnungen, Quellensteuer und Lohnausweisen.
Für Unternehmen bietet Payrolling eine effiziente Lösung zur Reduktion von administrativem Aufwand und zur Sicherstellung der Einhaltung aller gesetzlichen Vorschriften im Arbeitsrecht.
Was ist ein Spesenreglement?
Ein Spesenreglement ist eine schriftliche Richtlinie, die festlegt, welche Auslagen von Mitarbeitenden im Zusammenhang mit ihrer beruflichen Tätigkeit erstattet werden – z.â¯B. für Verpflegung, Fahrten, Übernachtungen oder Repräsentationskosten.
Ein klar formuliertes Spesenreglement schafft Transparenz, verhindert Missverständnisse und vereinfacht die Abrechnung gegenüber der Steuerverwaltung.
Wann muss ein Spesenreglement genehmigt werden?
Ein Spesenreglement muss von der zuständigen kantonalen Steuerbehörde genehmigt werden, wenn:
Pauschalspesen ausbezahlt werden sollen
das Unternehmen sicherstellen möchte, dass keine steuerpflichtigen Lohnbestandteile vorliegen
bei mehreren Geschäftsstellen oder komplexeren Strukturen
Ein genehmigtes Reglement bietet Rechtssicherheit und wird insbesondere bei einer Steuerprüfung als verbindliche Grundlage anerkannt.
Welche ArbeitsvertrÃ¤ge mÃ¼ssen schriftlich sein?
In der Schweiz gilt grundsätzlich Vertragsfreiheit – das heisst, ein Arbeitsvertrag kann auch mündlich abgeschlossen werden. Schriftlich erforderlich ist der Arbeitsvertrag jedoch in folgenden Fällen:
Lehrverträge (obligatorisch gemäss Berufsbildungsgesetz)
Befristete Arbeitsverträge mit Konkurrenzklausel oder Probezeitverlängerung
Regelungen zu Überstunden-, Ferien-, Lohn- oder Kündigungsmodalitäten, die vom Gesetz abweichen
Teilzeitverträge mit Quellensteuerpflichtigen Mitarbeitenden (aus Nachweisgründen)
Ein schriftlicher Vertrag schützt beide Parteien und schafft Klarheit.
Wie sind die gesetzlichen KÃ¼ndigungsfristen im Arbeitsrecht?
Die gesetzlichen Kündigungsfristen gemäss Art. 335c OR lauten wie folgt:
Während der Probezeit: 7 Tage auf beliebigen Termin
Im 1. Dienstjahr: 1 Monat auf Monatsende
Vom 2. bis zum 9. Dienstjahr: 2 Monate auf Monatsende
Ab dem 10. Dienstjahr: 3 Monate auf Monatsende
Abweichende Regelungen im Arbeitsvertrag oder Gesamtarbeitsvertrag sind zulässig, sofern sie schriftlich festgehalten werden. Die Einhaltung der Kündigungsfristen ist zentral für die Rechtssicherheit im Arbeitsverhältnis.

Nachfolgeregelung, Unternehmensbewertung und Umwandlung aus einer Hand
Eine frühzeitige und professionelle Planung rund um die Nachfolgeregelung sichert die Zukunft Ihres Unternehmens. Ob Sie Ihre Firma übergeben, umwandeln oder bewerten lassen möchten – mit unserer Unterstützung treffen Sie fundierte Entscheidungen.
Wir beraten Sie umfassend zur Nachfolgeregelung von KMU in der Schweiz, führen transparente Unternehmensbewertungen durch und begleiten Sie bei der Umwandlung Ihrer Firma in die passende Rechtsform.

Unterstützung in der Wirtschaftsberatung
bringt Sie weiter
LangjÃ¤hrige Erfahrung mit KMU
Unsere Expertise in der Nachfolgeregelung von KMU in der Schweiz basiert auf jahrelanger Praxis. Wir wissen, worauf es bei der Ãbergabe, Umwandlung und Bewertung wirklich ankommt.
Individuelle Beratung statt StandardlÃ¶sungen
Jedes Unternehmen ist einzigartig â genau wie seine Nachfolge oder Umstrukturierung. Wir erarbeiten massgeschneiderte LÃ¶sungen, die zu Ihrer Unternehmenssituation passen.
ZuverlÃ¤ssigkeit und Diskretion
Gerade in sensiblen Phasen wie einer Nachfolgeregelung oder Firmenumwandlung sind Vertrauen und Vertraulichkeit entscheidend. Darauf kÃ¶nnen Sie sich bei uns verlassen.
Unsere Leistungen im Bereich
Wirtschaftsberatung
Firmen-Umwandlungen
Die Umwandlung einer Firma â sei es die Umwandlung einer GmbH in eine AG, die Umwandlung einer Einzelfirma in eine GmbH oder gar die Umwandlung einer Einzelfirma in eine AG â ist ein bedeutender Schritt in der Unternehmensentwicklung. Damit dieser Ãbergang reibungslos und rechtssicher verlÃ¤uft, begleiten wir Sie mit fundierter Erfahrung und betriebswirtschaftlichem Know-how. Ob Sie wachsen, Investoren gewinnen oder Ihre Haftung neu regeln mÃ¶chten:
Wir analysieren Ihre Situation ganzheitlich und setzen die Umwandlung prÃ¤zise und effizient um. Profitieren Sie von einer persÃ¶nlichen Beratung, klaren Prozessen und unserer umfassenden Kenntnis des Schweizer Rechts- und Steuerumfelds. Wir stehen Ihnen zur Seite â von der ersten Idee bis zur erfolgreichen Umsetzung Ihrer Firmenumwandlung.
Unsere detaillierten Dienstleistungen
Eine Firmenumwandlung bringt nicht nur rechtliche, sondern auch steuerliche und buchhalterische Konsequenzen mit sich. Wir bieten Ihnen eine ganzheitliche Unterstützung – strukturiert, verständlich und auf Ihre Bedürfnisse abgestimmt.
Analyse der Ausgangslage und Wahl der passenden Rechtsform
Erstellung des Umwandlungsplans inkl. steuerlicher Beurteilung
Unterstützung bei der Statutenänderung und Handelsregisteranpassung
Kommunikation mit Behörden und Notariat
Übertragung von Aktiven und Passiven sowie Eröffnung der neuen Buchhaltung
Begleitung bei steuerlichen Vorabklärungen mit den zuständigen Behörden
So begleiten wir Ihre Firmenumwandlung Schritt fÃ¼r Schritt
Jede Unternehmensumwandlung ist individuell – dennoch folgen unsere Abläufe einem klaren Fahrplan. Wir koordinieren alle Beteiligten und stellen sicher, dass Ihre Interessen optimal vertreten sind.
Persönliche Erstberatung mit rechtlicher und steuerlicher Einschätzung
Klare Projektplanung inkl. Zeitrahmen und To-dos
Einfache Zusammenarbeit über digitale Tools und klaren Ansprechpartner
Laufende Information über den Fortschritt
Vollständige Begleitung bis zur finalen Eintragung im Handelsregister
Wissenswertes
Schon gewusst?
Bei der Umwandlung einer Einzelfirma in eine GmbH oder AG gilt es, auf eine saubere Übertragung der Aktiven und Passiven zu achten – inklusive einer Inventarliste und gegebenenfalls einer Gründungsprüfung. Zudem sind steuerliche Folgen frühzeitig abzuklären, da stille Reserven aufgelöst werden können. Ein frühzeitiges Gespräch lohnt sich.
Jetzt Offerte anfragen
Nachfolgeregelung
Die Nachfolgeregelung ist eine der wichtigsten strategischen Aufgaben fÃ¼r jedes Unternehmen â insbesondere fÃ¼r KMU in der Schweiz. Ob familienintern, durch Mitarbeitende oder externe KÃ¤ufer: Eine durchdachte Nachfolgeregelung fÃ¼r Ihr Unternehmen schafft Sicherheit, verhindert Konflikte und sorgt fÃ¼r einen reibungslosen Ãbergang. Wir begleiten Sie ganzheitlich â von der ersten Standortbestimmung bis zur erfolgreichen Ãbergabe.
Mit Erfahrung, FingerspitzengefÃ¼hl und einem strukturierten Vorgehen stellen wir sicher, dass Ihre Werte erhalten bleiben und Ihre Firma langfristig weitergefÃ¼hrt wird. Nutzen Sie unser Fachwissen in der Nachfolgeregelung fÃ¼r KMU und gestalten Sie die Zukunft Ihres Unternehmens aktiv mit.
Unsere detaillierten Dienstleistungen
Wir begleiten Sie bei jedem Schritt der Nachfolgeregelung – strategisch, rechtlich und emotional. Unser Ziel ist eine nachhaltige Lösung, die zu Ihnen, Ihrem Unternehmen und den Nachfolgerinnen und Nachfolgern passt.
Analyse der Ausgangslage und Entwicklung eines individuellen Nachfolgeplans
Unterstützung bei der Wahl zwischen interner und externer Nachfolge
Bewertung des Unternehmens (z.â¯B. für Kaufpreisfindung)
Klärung rechtlicher und steuerlicher Aspekte der Übergabe
Moderation von Gesprächen mit potenziellen Nachfolgern
Begleitung bis zur finalen Umsetzung inkl. Vertragsprüfung
So lÃ¤uft die Nachfolgeregelung bei uns ab
Eine gelungene Nachfolge beginnt mit Offenheit und Weitblick. Wir schaffen Klarheit über Ihre Optionen und begleiten Sie mit einem erprobten, pragmatischen Vorgehen.
Persönliches Erstgespräch zur Zieldefinition und Zeitplanung
Entwicklung eines klaren Fahrplans mit konkreten Meilensteinen
Einbindung von Fachpartnern (z.â¯B. Juristen, Steuerexperten)
Schrittweise Umsetzung – in Ihrem Tempo
Vertrauensvolle Begleitung bis zur Übergabe und darüber hinaus
Wissenswertes
Schon gewusst?
In der Schweiz steht bei rund jedem dritten KMU eine Nachfolgeregelung an – doch viele Unternehmerinnen und Unternehmer kümmern sich zu spät darum. Eine frühzeitige Planung, idealerweise 5 bis 10 Jahre vor der Übergabe, erhöht die Chancen auf eine erfolgreiche und steuerlich optimierte Nachfolge erheblich. Auch emotionale Aspekte wie Loslassen oder die Auswahl geeigneter Nachfolger spielen eine grosse Rolle – hier unterstützen wir Sie mit Erfahrung und Empathie.
Jetzt Offerte anfragen
Unternehmensbewertung
Eine Unternehmensbewertung ist in vielen Situationen entscheidend â sei es bei Nachfolgeplanungen, einem Verkauf, einer Fusion oder fÃ¼r strategische Entscheidungen. Wir bewerten Ihr Unternehmen unabhÃ¤ngig, fundiert und nachvollziehbar â unter BerÃ¼cksichtigung etablierter Methoden wie der Praktikermethode/Ertragswertmethode.
Ob Sie eine schnelle Orientierung oder eine detaillierte Analyse mit BerÃ¼cksichtigung aller relevanten Kennzahlen zur Unternehmensbewertung benÃ¶tigen: Wir liefern Ihnen belastbare Ergebnisse. Dank unserer langjÃ¤hrigen Erfahrung in der Unternehmensbewertung garantieren wir Ihnen Transparenz, PraxisnÃ¤he und eine individuelle Beratung â damit Sie die richtigen Entscheidungen treffen kÃ¶nnen.
Unsere detaillierten Dienstleistungen
Je nach Zielsetzung kombinieren wir bewährte Methoden und aktuelle Marktdaten mit einer betriebswirtschaftlichen Analyse. So erhalten Sie eine präzise, nachvollziehbare und rechtssichere Bewertung.
Durchführung der Unternehmensbewertung nach Praktikermethode
Anwendung des Ertragswertverfahrens / Ertragswertmethode
Bestimmung und Herleitung des Kapitalisierungssatzes
Analyse betriebswirtschaftlicher Kennzahlen und Branchenvergleich
Aufbereitung der Ergebnisse in einem strukturierten Bewertungsbericht
Persönliche Besprechung mit Handlungsempfehlungen
So lÃ¤uft Ihre Bewertung bei uns ab
Bei einer Unternehmensbewertung ist Transparenz entscheidend – sowohl im Vorgehen als auch in den Annahmen. Unsere strukturierte Herangehensweise macht den Bewertungsprozess nachvollziehbar und effizient.
Klärung des Bewertungsanlasses und Zielsetzung
Auswahl geeigneter Bewertungsmethoden (z.â¯B. Praktikermethode, Ertragswertverfahren)
Datenerhebung und Analyse der relevanten Finanzkennzahlen
Berechnung des Unternehmenswerts auf Basis objektiver Kriterien
Ergebnispräsentation mit Raum für Fragen und individuelle Perspektiven
Wissenswertes
Schon gewusst?
Die Wahl der Bewertungsmethode hängt vom Zweck der Bewertung ab: Für KMU hat sich in der Schweiz insbesondere die Praktikermethode etabliert, da sie sowohl Substanz- als auch Ertragswert kombiniert. Der verwendete Kapitalisierungssatz und die zugrunde gelegten Annahmen beeinflussen das Ergebnis wesentlich – deshalb ist eine professionelle Begleitung unerlässlich, um realistische und nachvollziehbare Werte zu erzielen.
Jetzt Offerte anfragen
HÃ¤ufig gestellte Fragen
Was ist eine Umwandlung eines Unternehmens?
Die Umwandlung eines Unternehmens bezeichnet die rechtliche Änderung der Unternehmensform – z.â¯B. von einer Einzelfirma in eine GmbH oder von einer GmbH in eine AG. Ziel ist oft eine bessere Haftungsbegrenzung, steuerliche Optimierung oder Vorbereitung auf eine Nachfolge oder Investition.
Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich begleitet Umwandlungen kompetent – von der Analyse der Ausgangslage bis zur notariellen Umsetzung und Anmeldung im Handelsregister.
Wie nennt man die Verschmelzung mehrere Unternehmen?
Die Verschmelzung mehrerer Unternehmen wird als Fusion bezeichnet. Dabei gehen zwei oder mehr Gesellschaften rechtlich ineinander über – entweder durch Absorption (eine übernimmt die andere) oder durch Kombination (neue Gesellschaft entsteht).
Fachgerechte steuerliche und rechtliche Planung ist essenziell. Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich unterstützt bei der Fusionsprüfung, Bewertung und Durchführung.
Wie berechnet man den Wert eines Unternehmens?
Der Unternehmenswert wird durch verschiedene Bewertungsverfahren berechnet – abhängig von Branche, Grösse und Zweck der Bewertung. Zu den gängigen Methoden zählen:
Substanzwertmethode: Ermittlung des Vermögens abzüglich Schulden
Ertragswertmethode: Zukunftserwartete Gewinne abgezinst auf den heutigen Wert
DCF-Methode (Discounted Cashflow): Zukunftsgerichtete Cashflows abzüglich Kapitalkosten
Multiplikatoren (EBIT-Multiples): Vergleich mit ähnlichen Unternehmen
Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich analysiert individuell und liefert eine fundierte Bewertung als Entscheidungsgrundlage.
Welche Formeln gibt es fÃ¼r die Unternehmensbewertung?
Einige häufig verwendete Formeln in der Unternehmensbewertung sind:
Ertragswert = (durchschnittlicher Jahresgewinn × Kapitalisierungsfaktor)
DCF-Wert = ∑ (Cashflowâ¯t / (1 + Diskontsatz)áµ)
Substanzwert = Vermögenswerte – Schulden
Multiplikatorverfahren = EBIT × Branchen-Multiple
Die Wahl der Formel hängt vom Bewertungsziel ab – ob Nachfolge, Verkauf oder Fusion.
Welche Kennzahlen sind fÃ¼r Unternehmensbewertung relevant?
Zu den wichtigsten Kennzahlen für die Bewertung eines Unternehmens gehören:
EBIT / EBITDA (operativer Gewinn vor Steuern und Abschreibungen)
Cashflow
Eigenkapitalquote
Umsatzentwicklung
Kundenbindung und Marktanteil
Finanzkennzahlen wie Verschuldungsgrad und Rentabilität
Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich analysiert diese Kennzahlen im Kontext und liefert praxisnahe Bewertungen.
Wie regle ich meine Nachfolge?
Eine erfolgreiche Unternehmensnachfolge beginnt mit einer frühzeitigen Planung. Folgende Schritte sind entscheidend:
Analyse der Ausgangslage
Festlegung der Ziele (familiäre, finanzielle, strategische)
Bewertung des Unternehmens
Suche nach interner oder externer Nachfolge
Rechtliche und steuerliche Strukturierung der Übergabe
Vertragsgestaltung und Umsetzung
Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich begleitet Nachfolgeregelungen ganzheitlich – diskret, professionell und lösungsorientiert.
Welche Arten der Unternehmensnachfolge gibt es?
In der Schweiz unterscheidet man hauptsächlich folgende Formen der Unternehmensnachfolge:
Familieninterne Nachfolge: Übergabe an Kinder oder Verwandte
Management Buy-out (MBO): Übernahme durch bestehendes Management
Management Buy-in (MBI): Übernahme durch externes Management
Verkauf an Dritte: Externer Käufer, z.â¯B. Investor oder Mitbewerber
Stiftungsmodell: Übergabe an eine gemeinnützige oder unternehmensnahe Stiftung
Jede Nachfolgeform bringt unterschiedliche steuerliche, rechtliche und emotionale Aspekte mit sich. Das Treuhand Zentrum Zürich hilft bei der optimalen Wahl und Umsetzung.

Vertrauen, Kompetenz und Weitsicht â das ist unser Versprechen
Seit Ã¼ber 17 Jahren begleiten wir Unternehmen und Privatpersonen mit massgeschneiderten Treuhand-, Steuer- und FinanzlÃ¶sungen. Unsere Kund:innen schÃ¤tzen unsere NÃ¤he, unser Netzwerk â und dass wir da sind, wenn die See stÃ¼rmt.
Jetzt kontaktieren
Ihr Kompetenzzentrum fÃ¼r Treuhand in ZÃ¼rich
Das Treuhandzentrum Zürich ist seit 2007 ein zuverlässiger Partner für Unternehmen und Privatpersonen. Unser breit aufgestelltes Dienstleistungsspektrum – von Treuhand über Steuern bis hin zu spezialisierten Services – basiert auf fundiertem Fachwissen, digitalisierten Prozessen und persönlicher Betreuung. Mit einem Standort in Zürich und einem Netzwerk nationaler wie internationaler Experten, bieten wir individuelle Lösungen, die perfekt auf die Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden abgestimmt sind.
Unser Ansatz:
Wir kombinieren modernste Technologien mit traditionellem Expertenwissen, um Ihnen eine 360°-Beratung zu bieten. Unser Ziel ist es, Prozesse zu optimieren, Transparenz zu schaffen und Ihnen so mehr Zeit für das Wesentliche zu geben – Ihr Kerngeschäft.
Worauf wir bauen
Ganzheitliche Kompetenz
Treuhand, Personaladministration, Recht und Steuern unter einem Dach â fÃ¼r effiziente, abgestimmte LÃ¶sungen.
VerlÃ¤ssliche Partnerschaft
Im Treuhandzentrum stehen wir fÃ¼r Vertrauen und BestÃ¤ndigkeit. Unsere Mandanten kÃ¶nnen sich darauf verlassen, dass wir ihre Anliegen mit hÃ¶chster Sorgfalt und Transparenz begleiten â heute und in Zukunft.
LÃ¶sungsorientierung
Herausforderungen begegnen wir mit einem klaren Blick nach vorn. Statt Probleme in den Vordergrund zu stellen, entwickeln wir pragmatische und passgenaue LÃ¶sungen, die echten Mehrwert schaffen.
Arbeiten beim Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich
Beim Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich arbeiten Menschen, die mehr wollen als nur Belege sortieren. Wir sind ein engagiertes Team aus TreuhÃ¤nder:innen, Steuerexpert:innen, Personalprofis und Beratenden â mit klarem Fokus auf QualitÃ¤t, Partnerschaft und Weiterentwicklung.

Bei uns zÃ¤hlen Eigenverantwortung, Mitdenken und gegenseitiger Respekt genauso wie ein gutes Miteinander im Alltag. Wir arbeiten strukturiert, digital und zukunftsorientiert â und trotzdem (oder gerade deswegen) mit Herz und Humor.

Succession Planning, Company Valuation, and Conversion from One Source
Early and professional planning around succession planning secures the future of your company. Whether you want to transfer your company, convert it, or have it valued â with our support, you make informed decisions.
We provide comprehensive consulting on succession planning for SMEs in Switzerland, conduct transparent company valuations, and accompany you in converting your company into the appropriate legal form.
Our Offer for Business Consulting
Company Conversions
Succession Planning
Company Valuation
Support in Business Consulting
Moves You Forward
Many Years of Experience with SMEs
Our expertise in succession planning for SMEs in Switzerland is based on years of practice. We know what really matters in transfer, conversion, and valuation.
Individual Consulting Instead of Standard Solutions
Every company is unique â just like its succession or restructuring. We develop tailored solutions that fit your company situation.
Reliability and Discretion
Especially in sensitive phases such as succession planning or company conversion, trust and confidentiality are crucial. You can rely on us for that.
Our Services in the Area of
Business Consulting
Company Conversions
Converting a company â whether converting a GmbH to an AG, converting a sole proprietorship to a GmbH, or even converting a sole proprietorship to an AG â is a significant step in company development. So this transition runs smoothly and in compliance with the law, we accompany you with sound experience and business management know-how. Whether you want to grow, gain investors, or reorganize your liability:
We analyze your situation holistically and implement the conversion precisely and efficiently. Benefit from personal consulting, clear processes, and our comprehensive knowledge of the Swiss legal and tax environment. We are at your side â from the first idea to the successful implementation of your company conversion.
Our Detailed Services
A company conversion brings not only legal but also tax and accounting consequences. We offer you comprehensive support – structured, understandable, and tailored to your needs.
Analysis of the starting situation and choice of the appropriate legal form
Preparation of the conversion plan incl. tax assessment
Support with articles of association changes and commercial register adjustment
Communication with authorities and notary
Transfer of assets and liabilities as well as opening of the new bookkeeping
Accompaniment in tax advance clarifications with the responsible authorities
How We Accompany Your Company Conversion Step by Step
Every company conversion is individual – nevertheless, our processes follow a clear roadmap. We coordinate all parties involved and ensure that your interests are optimally represented.
Personal initial consulting with legal and tax assessment
Clear project planning incl. timeframe and to-dos
Simple cooperation via digital tools and clear contact person
Ongoing information on progress
Complete accompaniment until final entry in the commercial register
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
When converting a sole proprietorship to a GmbH or AG, it is important to ensure a clean transfer of assets and liabilities – including an inventory list and, if applicable, a formation audit. In addition, tax consequences must be clarified early, as hidden reserves may be dissolved. An early conversation is worthwhile.
Request a quote now
Succession Planning
Succession planning is one of the most important strategic tasks for every company â especially for SMEs in Switzerland. Whether family-internal, through employees, or external buyers: A well-thought-out succession planning for your company creates security, prevents conflicts, and ensures a smooth transition. We accompany you comprehensively â from the first assessment to the successful handover.
With experience, sensitivity, and a structured approach, we ensure that your values are preserved and your company is continued long-term. Use our expertise in succession planning for SMEs and actively shape the future of your company.
Our Detailed Services
We accompany you at every step of succession planning – strategically, legally, and emotionally. Our goal is a sustainable solution that fits you, your company, and the successors.
Analysis of the starting situation and development of an individual succession plan
Support with choice between internal and external succession
Company valuation (e.g., for purchase price determination)
Clarification of legal and tax aspects of the handover
Moderation of conversations with potential successors
Accompaniment until final implementation incl. contract review
How Succession Planning Works with Us
A successful succession begins with openness and foresight. We create clarity about your options and accompany you with a proven, pragmatic approach.
Personal initial conversation for goal definition and time planning
Development of a clear roadmap with concrete milestones
Involvement of specialist partners (e.g., lawyers, tax experts)
Step-by-step implementation – at your pace
Trustworthy accompaniment until handover and beyond
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
In Switzerland, about one in three SMEs faces succession planning – but many entrepreneurs take care of it too late. Early planning, ideally 5 to 10 years before the handover, significantly increases the chances of a successful and tax-optimized succession. Emotional aspects such as letting go or selecting suitable successors also play a major role – here we support you with experience and empathy.
Request a quote now
Company Valuation
A company valuation is crucial in many situations â whether succession planning, a sale, a merger, or for strategic decisions. We evaluate your company independently, soundly, and comprehensibly â taking into account established methods such as the practitioner method/earnings value method.
Whether you need a quick orientation or a detailed analysis considering all relevant key figures for company valuation: We deliver reliable results. Thanks to our many years of experience in company valuation, we guarantee you transparency, practical relevance, and individual consulting â so you can make the right decisions.
Our Detailed Services
Depending on the objective, we combine proven methods and current market data with a business management analysis. This gives you a precise, comprehensible, and legally secure valuation.
Conducting company valuation according to practitioner method
Application of earnings value procedure / earnings value method
Determination and derivation of the capitalization rate
Analysis of business management key figures and industry comparison
Preparation of results in a structured valuation report
Personal discussion with recommendations for action
How Your Valuation Works with Us
In a company valuation, transparency is crucial – both in approach and in assumptions. Our structured approach makes the valuation process comprehensible and efficient.
Clarification of valuation occasion and objective
Selection of suitable valuation methods (e.g., practitioner method, earnings value procedure)
Data collection and analysis of relevant financial key figures
Calculation of company value based on objective criteria
Result presentation with room for questions and individual perspectives
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
The choice of valuation method depends on the purpose of the valuation: For SMEs, the practitioner method has particularly established itself in Switzerland, as it combines both asset and earnings value. The capitalization rate used and the underlying assumptions significantly influence the result – that is why professional accompaniment is essential to achieve realistic and comprehensible values.
Request a quote now
Frequently Asked Questions
What is a company conversion?
Company conversion refers to the legal change of the company form â e.g., from a sole proprietorship to a GmbH or from a GmbH to an AG. The goal is often better liability limitation, tax optimization, or preparation for succession or investment.
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich competently accompanies conversions â from analyzing the starting situation to notarial implementation and registration in the commercial register.
What is the merger of several companies called?
The merger of several companies is called a fusion. Here, two or more companies legally merge â either through absorption (one takes over the other) or through combination (a new company is created).
Professional tax and legal planning is essential. Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich supports with fusion review, valuation, and implementation.
How is the value of a company calculated?
Company value is calculated through various valuation methods â depending on industry, size, and purpose of the valuation. Common methods include:
Asset value method: Determining assets minus liabilities
Earnings value method: Future expected profits discounted to present value
DCF method (Discounted Cashflow): Future-oriented cash flows minus capital costs
Multipliers (EBIT multiples): Comparison with similar companies
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich analyzes individually and provides a sound valuation as a basis for decision-making.
What formulas are there for company valuation?
Some commonly used formulas in company valuation are:
Earnings value = (average annual profit Ã capitalization factor)
DCF value = Î£ (Cashflow t / (1 + discount rate)áµ)
Asset value = Assets â Liabilities
Multiplier method = EBIT Ã Industry multiple
The choice of formula depends on the valuation objective â whether succession, sale, or fusion.
Which key figures are relevant for company valuation?
The most important key figures for valuing a company include:
EBIT / EBITDA (operating profit before taxes and depreciation)
Cash flow
Equity ratio
Revenue development
Customer loyalty and market share
Financial key figures such as debt ratio and profitability
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich analyzes these key figures in context and provides practical valuations.
How do I plan my succession?
A successful company succession begins with early planning. The following steps are crucial:
Analysis of the starting situation
Determination of goals (family, financial, strategic)
Company valuation
Search for internal or external succession
Legal and tax structuring of the transfer
Contract design and implementation
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich accompanies succession planning comprehensively â discreetly, professionally, and solution-oriented.
What types of company succession are there?
In Switzerland, the following forms of company succession are mainly distinguished:
Family-internal succession: Transfer to children or relatives
Management Buy-out (MBO): Takeover by existing management
Management Buy-in (MBI): Takeover by external management
Sale to third parties: External buyer, e.g., investor or competitor
Foundation model: Transfer to a non-profit or company-related foundation
Each succession form brings different tax, legal, and emotional aspects. Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich helps with optimal choice and implementation.

Reliable Bookkeeping for Sustainable Business Success
A well-organized financial bookkeeping system is the foundation of every successful company. Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich handles all tasks related to your bookkeeping, from ongoing financial bookkeeping to preparing annual financial statements to processing VAT returns.
Our professional trustees in Zurich help you maintain an overview of your cash flow and liquidity and enable you to make informed decisions through precise planning calculations and key performance indicators.
Our Offer in Bookkeeping and Financial Statements
Financial Bookkeeping
Annual Financial Statements
VAT
Support in Bookkeeping
Moves You Forward
Individual Financial Planning & Controlling
We create tailored financial plans and liquidity plans to secure your financial stability long-term. This allows you to respond early to fluctuations in cash flow and take timely action.
Efficient Payment Processing
We handle your accounts payable and accounts receivable bookkeeping including payment processing. This ensures that your invoices are paid on time and outstanding receivables are consistently collected.
Transparent Management Accounting
Our management accounting provides you with detailed insights into your business figures. Through regular monthly closing and key performance indicator comparisons, you always know where your company stands and can optimize accordingly.
Our Services in the Area of
Bookkeeping and Financial Statements
Financial Bookkeeping
Well-managed financial bookkeeping is essential for SMEs, self-employed individuals, and startups. It creates clarity about your financial situation and forms the foundation for successful decisions. As an established trust services firm in Zurich, we handle your complete financial bookkeeping so you can fully concentrate on your core business. Our experienced trustees in Zurich support you from ongoing bookkeeping to accounts receivable and accounts payable bookkeeping to the preparation of monthly financial statements.
We place particular emphasis on liquidity planning so you always have sufficient cash flow available. With targeted planning calculations and key performance indicator comparisons, we also ensure efficient controlling that makes your company development transparent and facilitates future financial planning.
Our Services in Financial Bookkeeping
Benefit from comprehensive services that make your daily business easier:
Ongoing recording of all business transactions
Preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
Accounts receivable and accounts payable bookkeeping incl. payment and collection
Liquidity planning and cash flow analysis
Management accounting and controlling
Financial planning and key performance indicator comparisons
How Your Bookkeeping Works with TZZ
Efficient financial bookkeeping requires clear processes and smooth communication. As your trustee in Zurich, we rely on digital solutions and transparent processes that save you time and provide clarity.
Submit receipts digitally:
No more paperwork, we work paperless.
Access to current reports:
Your financial data is available online at any time.
Proactive liquidity planning:
early recognition of bottlenecks and options for action.
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
Retention obligations: Business documents must be kept for at least 10 years according to Swiss law.
Liquidity security: Professional liquidity planning protects your company from financial bottlenecks and unexpected costs.
Optimization potential: Regular analysis of your key figures helps to recognize savings potential early and make strategic decisions optimally.
Request a quote now
Annual Financial Statements
Annual financial statements are more than just a mandatory task for SMEs, startups, and self-employed individuals in Switzerland: They are a valuable management tool that creates clarity about the financial situation and future prospects of your company. Our trust services experts support you in preparing legally compliant annual financial statements and interim statements that transparently document your entrepreneurial success.
With carefully prepared annual financial statements, you receive not only a reliable basis for your tax return but also important information for strategic decisions and investments. Trust our experience and competence to ensure that your annual financial statements meet all legal requirements and at the same time serve as an efficient controlling tool.
Our Services for Annual Financial Statements
Our specialized trustees create comprehensive annual and interim financial statements for your company with the following services:
Preparation of proper annual financial statements according to Swiss
Accounting standards
Preparation of interim statements for mid-year assessment
Consulting and support with balance sheet and income statement analysis
Preparation of notes and reports according to legal requirements
Preparation of annual financial statements for tax returns
How Your Annual Financial Statements Work with TZZ
In preparing your annual financial statements, we place particular emphasis on accuracy, transparency, and timely communication. Our goal is straightforward cooperation that saves you administrative effort.
Personal Contact Person:
individual support through our experts.
Digital Cooperation:
simple and secure transmission of all relevant documents.
Transparent Communication:
regular updates on the status of your annual financial statements.
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
In Switzerland, annual financial statements according to the Code of Obligations (OR) must be prepared and kept no later than six months after the end of the fiscal year. Timely preparation avoids unnecessary stress and helps minimize tax risks.
Request a quote now
VAT
Correct processing of VAT is essential for SMEs, self-employed individuals, and startups in Switzerland to avoid financial risks and reliably meet legal requirements. Our experienced team handles the complete VAT return for you and ensures that you meet all requirements on time and in compliance with the law.
We prepare periodic VAT returns, review your business transactions for correct tax treatment, and support you with all questions around VAT in Switzerland. Benefit from our expertise and focus worry-free on your core business while we reliably process your VAT.
Our Detailed Services
Our services around VAT returns cover all essential areas that relieve and secure you:
Periodic preparation of VAT returns
Review and recording of VAT
Support with VAT audits and reviews
Consulting on VAT optimization
Timely filing of VAT returns with authorities
How Your VAT Return Works with TZZ
Cooperation around VAT returns is simple, transparent, and digital. Our processes are designed for efficiency and reliability to ensure maximum security for you at all times.
Digital Transmission:
upload receipts conveniently online
Personal Consulting:
fixed contact person for all your VAT questions
Punctual Submission:
we ensure that all deadlines are met
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
Companies with an annual turnover of at least CHF 100,000 are required to register with the Federal Tax Administration and regularly file VAT returns. Non-compliance with deadlines can result in back payments and fines.
Request a quote now
Frequently Asked Questions
How often should I create a liquidity plan?
A liquidity plan should be updated regularly, ideally monthly, to recognize fluctuations early and be able to respond.
What is the difference between financial bookkeeping and management accounting?
Financial bookkeeping documents all business transactions and provides important figures for external reports. Management accounting, on the other hand, analyzes internal costs and revenues and supports internal decision-making.
Why should I outsource financial bookkeeping to a trust services firm?
By outsourcing your bookkeeping to a professional trust services firm, you save resources, reduce sources of error, and benefit from specialized expertise.
What documents do I need for the VAT return?
For the VAT return, we need all invoices and receipts that show VAT as well as an overview of your income and expenses.
What does a trustee do in Switzerland?
A trustee in Switzerland takes on diverse tasks in the areas of bookkeeping, taxes, business consulting, and legal support. They help private individuals as well as companies to fulfill their financial and administrative obligations correctly, efficiently, and in compliance with the law. This includes, among other things, managing bookkeeping, preparing annual financial statements, tax returns, payroll administration, and consulting on company formations, restructuring, or succession planning. Through sound expertise and knowledge of current legislation, a trustee offers not only administrative relief but also strategic consulting â for example, on optimizing tax burden or long-term financial planning.

Whether payroll accounting, employment contracts, or expense reports: Correct HR administration is essential for every company.

Moves You Forward
Practical and Efficient
Our solutions are tailored to Swiss SMEs â understandable, clearly structured, and immediately implementable. Whether payroll accounting or expense regulations â we deliver practice-tested templates and individual consulting.
Legally Secure and Current
We keep your employment contracts Switzerland-compliant and your expense guidelines up to date. This avoids unnecessary risks and conflicts.
Individual Support
With us, you have fixed contact persons â from Payroll Specialist to legal consulting. We take time for your questions and develop sustainable solutions together.
Our Services in the Area of
HR Administration
Payroll Accounting (Payroll)
Correct and punctual payroll accounting is central for every company â whether startup, SME, or sole proprietorship. We handle your complete payroll accounting: reliably, legally compliant, and efficiently. As payroll specialists, we know the particularities of payroll accounting in Switzerland and consider individual topics such as bonus payments, withholding tax, or social contributions. With our support, you save time, avoid errors, and create trust with your employees.
Our Detailed Services
Whether individual wages or complex payroll structures – we offer you a tailored solution for your payroll accounting.
Monthly payroll statements
Processing and reporting of social insurance (AHV, BVG, UVG, etc.)
Withholding tax processing for affected employees
Preparation of payroll journals and annual reports
Support with wage certificate preparation and audits
Registration and deregistration of employees with insurance and authorities
How Your Payroll Works with Us
With us, you maintain an overview – without having to fight through complicated payroll guidelines. Our digital cooperation enables you to access the relevant documents at any time – efficiently, securely, and location-independent.
Submit receipts and payroll data conveniently
Access to current reports and payroll journals
Personal support through experienced Payroll Specialists
Security through compliance with all legal requirements
Flexible adaptation in case of personnel changes or wage increases
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
In Switzerland, companies must issue employees a correct wage certificate annually – including all relevant wage components and deductions. Incorrect information can not only lead to inquiries from the tax office but also to financial disadvantages. Proper payroll accounting by a Payroll Specialist helps avoid these risks and ensures trust with both your employees and authorities.
Request a quote now
Expense Regulations
Expenses are a frequent topic â and without clear regulation often a source of uncertainty. We help you create a legally secure, transparent expense regulation that fits your company. From expense regulation templates to the question of whether expenses are subject to social contributions. We advise you comprehensively and ensure a tax-compliant solution. This way you maintain an overview, avoid inquiries from authorities, and offer your employees fair framework conditions.
Our Detailed Services
A well-designed expense regulation saves you time, discussions, and possible back payments by authorities.
Creation and adaptation of expense regulations according to Swiss standards
Consulting on tax treatment of expenses (incl. VAT and BVG)
Provision and individualization of an expense regulation template
Review of existing regulations for legal compliance
Training or information of employees on expense processing
Support with digital recording and documentation of expenses
Creating an Expense Regulation Correctly
With a professionally created expense regulation, you ensure that all employees know the same rules – and your payroll accounting remains legally secure.
Clear definition of expense-eligible outlays
Clear and fair regulations for all employees
Security on the question "Which expenses are subject to BVG?"
Audit-proof documentation through digital processes
Integration of the regulation into your existing HR administration
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
Expenses are only tax-free and not subject to social contributions if they are correctly documented and paid within the framework of an approved expense regulation. Swiss tax authorities only recognize regulations that are clearly formulated, internally communicated, and consistently implemented. That is why it is important not only to create the regulation but also to regularly review and adapt it if necessary.
Request a quote now
Employment Contracts
A clearly formulated employment contract protects both sides. We support you in creating and reviewing your employment contracts â understandably, legally secure, and tailored to your company. Whether fixed-term, part-time, or with home office arrangements. We ensure that your employment contracts in Switzerland meet all legal requirements. Benefit from our experience in HR administration and create a stable foundation for a good employment relationship with each contract.
Our Detailed Services
From creation to adaptation to review of existing employment contracts – we offer you comprehensive support with a focus on Swiss law.
Creation of individual employment contracts for various types of employment
Review and revision of existing contracts
Consulting on legal requirements and industry-specific regulations
Sample contracts and templates tailored to your company
Regulation of special topics such as home office, variable wages, or expenses
Support with contract changes or termination agreements
Creating an Employment Contract Correctly
With TZZ at your side, you create employment contracts that are convincing both legally and organizationally – clear, transparent, and efficient.
Legally secure contracts based on current legislation
Individual adaptation to your company structure and industry
Support with special cases such as internships or freelancer contracts
Quick implementation thanks to proven processes and templates
Personal contact person for your HR administration
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
In Switzerland, contract freedom applies in principle – but numerous labor law regulations (e.g., OR, GAV, data protection) set clear framework conditions. Inaccurate or missing information in the employment contract can lead to legal conflicts. That is why it is worth relying on professional support – especially for first-time employment or changes in the company.
Request a quote now
Employment Law
Questions around employment law in Switzerland confront companies continuously â whether terminations, overtime, or vacation. So you are legally on the safe side, we work with experienced lawyers from our partner network on all employment law matters. You receive sound answers combined with our practical implementation in HR administration. This way we combine legal expertise with efficient consulting â individually and reliably.
Our Detailed Services
We accompany you with our experience – and draw in specialized professionals from our network if needed.
Initial assessment of employment law questions from everyday life
Support with legally secure implementation of working time and vacation regulations
Consulting on contract terminations and dismissals
Cooperation with specialist lawyers for Swiss employment law in complex cases
Adaptation of your internal documents according to employment law requirements
Creating an Employment Contract Correctly
Legal clarity creates trust – internally and externally. We accompany you reliably and refer you to the appropriate experts if needed.
Reliable first contact for all questions around employment law
Access to our proven partner network with specialists
Combination of legal expertise and administrative implementation
Fast response times for urgent cases
Documents and processes up to date with Swiss employment law
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
In Switzerland, the Code of Obligations (OR) applies as the basis of employment law. Additionally, depending on the industry, collective agreements (GAV), cantonal regulations, or company-internal regulations apply. A legally clean foundation protects you in case of disputes – whether terminations, warnings, or disputes around overtime. Our cooperation with external specialist lawyers ensures that you always act correctly and comprehensibly.
Request a quote now
Frequently Asked Questions
What does payroll accounting include?
Payroll accounting includes all activities related to employee compensation. This includes:
Calculation and payment of wages
Processing of social insurance (AHV, IV, ALV, BVG, UVG)
Withholding tax returns
Preparation of payroll statements and wage certificates
Management of vacation, overtime, and absences
Year-end processing and notifications to authorities
Correct payroll accounting is crucial for compliance with legal requirements and employee trust. Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich handles these tasks efficiently and reliably.
What is the difference between bookkeeping and payroll accounting?
Bookkeeping reflects the overall financial situation of a company. It includes the recording of business transactions such as income, expenses, investments, and the preparation of balance sheets and income statements.
Payroll accounting is a specialized area of bookkeeping. It relates exclusively to managing wages and processing all payroll-related social contributions and returns.
In short: Bookkeeping manages finances, payroll accounting manages personnel â both belong to the core business of a trustee.
What is payrolling?
Payrolling means the complete outsourcing of payroll administration to an external service provider such as Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich. The trustee takes over the entire payroll processing including payroll statements, social insurance returns, withholding tax, and wage certificates.
For companies, payrolling offers an efficient solution to reduce administrative effort and ensure compliance with all legal requirements in labor law.
What is an expense regulation?
An expense regulation is a written guideline that determines which expenses of employees in connection with their professional activity are reimbursed â e.g., for meals, travel, accommodations, or representation costs.
A clearly formulated expense regulation creates transparency, prevents misunderstandings, and simplifies returns to the tax administration.
When must an expense regulation be approved?
An expense regulation must be approved by the responsible cantonal tax authority if:
Flat-rate expenses are to be paid out
the company wants to ensure that there are no taxable wage components
with multiple business locations or more complex structures
An approved regulation offers legal security and is particularly recognized as a binding basis in a tax audit.
Which employment contracts must be in writing?
In Switzerland, contract freedom applies in principle â meaning an employment contract can also be concluded orally. However, a written employment contract is required in the following cases:
Apprenticeship contracts (mandatory under the Vocational Training Act)
Fixed-term employment contracts with non-compete clauses or probation period extensions
Regulations on overtime, vacation, wages, or termination modalities that deviate from the law
Part-time contracts with employees subject to withholding tax (for documentation purposes)
A written contract protects both parties and creates clarity.
What are the legal notice periods in employment law?
The legal notice periods according to Art. 335c OR are as follows:
During probation period: 7 days on any date
In the 1st year of service: 1 month at month-end
From the 2nd to the 9th year of service: 2 months at month-end
From the 10th year of service: 3 months at month-end
Deviating regulations in the employment contract or collective agreement are permissible if they are recorded in writing. Compliance with notice periods is central to legal security in the employment relationship.

Form GmbH, AG, or Sole Proprietorship â We Make It Easy
Those who want to start a company in Switzerland have many questions â from legal requirements to costs. We accompany founders, startups, and SMEs in choosing the right legal form and take care of all formalities.
Whether you want to form a GmbH, build an AG, or start a sole proprietorship â with our support, the start becomes easier, faster, and safer.
Our Offer for Company Formations
Form Sole Proprietorship
Company Formation GmbH
Company Formation Stock Corporation (AG)
Company Formation Other Legal Forms
Your
Company Formation
in Good Hands
Formation with System
We know the processes and requirements of Swiss authorities and know what matters in a successful formation â whether you are forming a start-up or starting a sole proprietorship.
Individual Consulting
Every formation is different. We help you choose the right legal form, explain advantages and disadvantages, and support you in all necessary steps â competently and practically.
Fast and Transparent
We tell you exactly which documents we need, what the costs for a GmbH formation are, and how long the process takes. This way you always maintain an overview.
Our Services in the Area of
Company Formations
Form Sole Proprietorship
The sole proprietorship is the simplest way to officially start a self-employed activity. It is ideally suited for persons who want to get started without large start-up capital. Those who want to form a sole proprietorship benefit from quick formation, few formalities, and full control. 

So everything runs correctly from the start, we accompany you step by step: from registration to entry. We clarify your obligations, take care of registrations with offices, and show when it might be worthwhile to form a GmbH long-term.
Our Detailed Services
You bring the idea – we take over the administrative part so you can start directly.
Consulting on requirements and differences to GmbH
Preparation of necessary registrations and documents
Entry in commercial register (if desired or necessary)
Registration with AHV, VAT, and further offices
Support with choosing company name and activity
Tax initial consulting and look at next steps
How We Accompany You When Starting Your Sole Proprietorship
Simply start – without uncertainty. We make the formation of your sole proprietorship efficient and clearly comprehensible.
Personal consulting conversation for optimal preparation
We take over all forms and official procedures
Clear overview of rights, obligations, and possible hurdles
Tips for bookkeeping and invoicing from the start
Possibility to later convert into a GmbH, AG
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
Those who want to form a sole proprietorship need no start-up capital. However, from an annual turnover of CHF 100,000, there is an obligation to register for VAT. Even if no commercial register entry is prescribed, it can be useful – such as for better visibility or for company name protection. We show you what makes most sense in your situation.
Request a quote now
Company Formation GmbH
The GmbH is one of the most popular legal forms in Switzerland â especially for founders who appreciate a clear structure and limited liability. If you want to form a GmbH, we accompany you from A to Z. We not only help you choose the appropriate legal form but also take care of all legal, financial, and administrative steps. Whether you want to start a start-up or convert an existing company into a GmbH â with our experience, we ensure that you start optimally. Thanks to our comprehensive support, you avoid typical stumbling blocks when forming a company in Switzerland and always maintain an overview of time, costs, and obligations.
Our Services for GmbH Formation
From idea to commercial register entry – we competently and efficiently accompany your GmbH formation.
These services are included:
Consulting on choosing the appropriate legal form and structure
Preparation of formation documents incl. articles of association
Coordination with notary and commercial register
Registration with AHV compensation fund and VAT (if necessary)
Support with opening a capital contribution account
Tax initial consulting
How Your Formation Works with TZZ
Those who want to form a company face many small but decisive steps. We guide you structured through the entire process – transparent, efficient, and individually tailored to your situation.
Your advantages and the typical process:
Personal consulting with individual needs analysis
All documents legally secure and timely prepared
Full transparency on GmbH formation costs
Clear checklists and ongoing updates on status
Follow-up support with bookkeeping & tax consulting
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
For forming a GmbH in Switzerland, minimum capital of CHF 20,000 is required. This must be fully paid in before entry in the commercial register – either on a capital contribution account or through contributions in kind. Important: Compliance with formal requirements is crucial, as missing or incorrect documents can lead to delays or rejections.
Request a quote now
Company Formation Stock Corporation (AG)
Those who want to form an AG usually plan long-term. The stock corporation is particularly suitable for founders with growth in mind â such as start-ups with investor plans or companies with multiple shareholders. We personally accompany you through the entire process: from the first idea to entry in the commercial register. 

Forming your own company in Switzerland brings many questions â we provide the answers, take care of the formalities, and ensure a smooth start. This leaves more time for what matters: your vision.
Our Services for AG Formation
So you can focus on your business model, we take over all necessary steps around the formation.
Personal consulting on choosing structure and share distribution
Preparation of formation documents incl. articles of association
Coordination with notary and commercial register office
Support with capital contribution and account opening
Registration with AHV, VAT, and further authorities
Tax initial consulting incl. location choice and participation structure
How Your AG Formation Works
Clarity, efficiency, and a partner who thinks along – that makes the difference when forming an AG.
Initial consulting with focus on your entrepreneurial goals
All documents and appointments organized from one source
Clear overview of steps, deadlines, and formation costs
Quick processing thanks to established network
Further support with bookkeeping, payroll & taxes on request
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
For forming an AG, minimum capital of CHF 100,000 is required. At least CHF 50,000 of this must be paid in at formation. The AG is particularly suitable if multiple persons are involved or external investors are to join. Important: Even non-listed AGs must comply with certain publication and audit obligations – we show you what matters.
Request a quote now
Company Formation Other Legal Forms
Not every company fits into the classic scheme of AG, GmbH, or sole proprietorship. Depending on business purpose, size, or participation structure, other legal forms such as general partnership, foundation, association, or limited partnership can also make sense. We help you choose the appropriate structure.

Whether you want to start with partners, build a non-profit organization, or restructure an existing company â we ensure that everything is correctly and future-proof set up.
Our Detailed Services
You receive comprehensive consulting and implementation from us – exactly tailored to your needs and plans.
Analysis of your starting situation and consulting on suitable legal form
Preparation of individual articles of association or partnership agreements
Coordination with notary, commercial register, and authorities
Registration with social insurance, tax authorities, VAT, etc.
Support with internal regulations (e.g., with associations or foundations)
Optional: Long-term support with bookkeeping, taxes, and law
How We Work with Special Formation Forms
Formations outside the standard require experience and a good feel for individual requirements.
Clear recommendations on which structure fits your goals
Tailored documents and smooth process
Support with internal processes and obligations
Transparent listing of all necessary steps and costs
Personal contact and reliable support
Interesting facts
Did You Know?
Depending on the chosen legal form, different requirements apply – such as in bookkeeping, liability, or representation externally. With foundations and associations, special articles of association or regulations are often required. Also with companies with multiple participants, a cleanly formulated contract is crucial. We ensure that you not only start legally correctly but are also organizationally well positioned.
Request a quote now
Frequently Asked Questions
What is meant by startup?
A startup is a young, growth-oriented company that pursues an innovative business idea and is still in the build-up or development phase. Startups are often characterized by high growth potential, a scalable idea, and a certain financial risk.
What is the difference between a startup and a company?
The main difference lies in the phase and orientation:
A startup is in the initial phase, searches for a viable business model, and often focuses on innovation and rapid growth.
A company can already be established, pursues a stable business model, and is solidified in its structure and organization.
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich accompanies both startups and established companies â with individual solutions and comprehensive consulting.
How much start-up capital does an AG need?
For forming an Aktiengesellschaft (AG) in Switzerland, minimum capital of CHF 100,000.â is required, of which at least CHF 50,000.â must be paid in. An AG is particularly suitable for startups with expansion plans or investor participation.
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich accompanies you in forming your AG â from capital planning to commercial register entry.
What is the best legal form for a startup?
The optimal legal form depends on factors such as capital, liability, growth prospects, and number of founders. For startups, the following are particularly suitable:
GmbH: Ideal with 1â3 founders, with clear relationships and limited liability
AG: With greater capital needs, external investors, or scaling goals
Sole proprietorship: Fast and cost-effective â but with personal liability
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich analyzes your situation and recommends the appropriate legal form â taking into account taxes, liability, and growth.
What does a GmbH formation cost?
The formation of a GmbH costs in Switzerland depending on effort and services:
Notary costs and commercial register entry: approx. CHF 600.â to CHF 1,200.â
Capital requirement: at least CHF 20,000.â fully paid in
Trust services costs for consulting & processing: from CHF 500.â (depending on complexity)
Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich offers transparent formation packages, including articles of association, entry, VAT registration, and consulting on social insurance.
How much start-up capital do you need for a startup?
The required start-up capital depends strongly on the industry, business model, and chosen legal form. As guidelines:
Sole proprietorship: from CHF 0.â possible (no capital contribution prescribed)
GmbH: at least CHF 20,000.â (fully paid in)
AG: at least CHF 100,000.â (at least CHF 50,000.â paid in)
Additionally, working capital, reserves, and investments should be planned. Treuhandzentrum ZÃ¼rich supports you in realistic financial planning and capital procurement.

Insurance matters, but not every policy is suitable at every stage. We advise startups independently, explain risks clearly, and prioritise coverage that fits your business model, budget, and growth phase. We do not broker or sell products.

AHV/IV/EO, ALV, UVG, and BVG â the correct setup depends on legal form, activities, and team. We plan a social insurance solution that grows with your company and complies with Swiss requirements.
Common questions concern classification (employee vs. selfâemployed), contributions, and deadlines. We bring clarity and establish practical processes.
Our Services

Correct classification (employee vs. selfâemployed) determines contributions and benefits – misclassification often leads to back payments.
Selfâemployed contributions follow a degressive system; the compensation office assesses status case by case.
Careful documentation and onâtime filings reduce audit and backâpayment risks.

Startups & VAT (MWST)
From registration duty to rates and returns: we set up your VAT topics so processes are understandable, auditable, and practical â from early stage to scaleâup.

Design of invoicing and booking processes (e.g., SaaS, eâcommerce, B2B/B2C, foreign elements)
Preparation of VAT returns (e.g., quarterly) and support with input tax deduction
Support with VAT audits and appeals
Our Approach
Liability check including threshold monitoring and documented assessment.
Alignment with accounting: correct coding, document flow, and rate application.
Review before submission and a concise management summary.
Interesting facts
Did you know?
Current Swiss VAT rates (as of 2024/2025): 8.1% (standard), 2.6% (reduced), 3.8% (accommodation).
VAT liability is generally based on worldwide turnover – exceptions may apply; caseâbyâcase assessment is key.
SaaS and platform models require clear rules on place of supply and documentation.

Does Treuhandzentrum broker or sell insurance products?
No. We provide independent advice and do not broker products. We prepare decision documents and, if desired, support your discussions with providers.
When does VAT liability apply in Switzerland?

Who decides on âemployeeâ vs âselfâemployedâ status?
The competent compensation office assesses status case by case. Classification affects contributions and benefits.
Do you support registrations and filings (VAT, AHV, etc.)?
Yes. We handle registrations/communications with authorities (e.g., FTA/ESTV, compensation offices) and support with filings and audits.
